verdict_reasoning: EREG (epiregulin) is an EGF-family ligand encoded as a single-pass type I transmembrane proligand (pro-EREG). The TM precursor is processed by ADAM17/sheddases at the cell surface to release the soluble mature growth factor, which is the dominant biological actor signaling through EGFR/ERBB4. The TM pro-form does transit the plasma membrane and can mediate juxtacrine signaling, but it is short-lived relative to the shed soluble pool. This matches the dual_localization pattern for transient TM proligands of shed-ligand-dominant genes.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Surface residence time of pro-EREG TM precursor vs rate of ADAM17-mediated shedding is not precisely quantified.
